topic: Antarctic gravity hole — post-EXP-215 IF-THEN reframe + geographic anchor + linear-coupling disclosure
change_summary: EXP-215 integrated 5 patches post-c7: (1) finding now discloses linear-coupling caveat, (2) tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict propagate the caveat plus 70°S geographic anchor with explicit 5,600× interior-comparison disclosure, (3) detail_evidence replaces the bald g=f(H(r)) assertion with an IF-THEN conditional routed through dome raw-text quotations (Domain 4 dielectric-pressure/aetheric-density language, disc-edge pressure-gradient quote from wins.txt, shared 8,619 km decay constant), (4) detail_extra paragraph (ii) relabels the 9.826 m/s² anchor as ~70°S (Antarctic coast/Southern Ocean transition) with a conservative-anchor disclosure and 5,600× interior ratio, and (5) retains the 170× dimensionless lower bound. The patch directly closes c6 holes #2, #3, #4 and c7 holes #2–#4. Carry-forward unaddressed: c7 hole #1 (in-detail 1,430× vs 170× asymmetry), c7 hole #6 (arithmetic precision slip from 0.006 rounding), c6 hole #5 (Shepherd 2012 citation).
our_argument_summary: The dome model’s own exponential height function H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) km collapses from 8,537 km at the pole to 837 km at the Antarctic rim (a 10× drop). The dome does NOT explicitly publish the sentence g = f(H(r)), but it (a) names the Domain 4 gravity mechanism “Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation” and (b) chooses the Domain 4 gravity decay length λg = 8,619 km identical to the H(r) decay constant. Under an IF-THEN conditional that takes the dome narrative at face value, any monotonic g↔H(r) coupling predicts a large gravity drop at the rim — 90% under linear, 38% under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling. Observed Antarctic-latitude gravity (9.826 m/s² at ~70°S, with a deliberate choice of the defender’s conservative anchor) is just 0.0062 m/s² below the polar reference — a 1,430× apples-to-apples error under linear coupling; ~590× under H^(1/5); ~5,600× if we re-anchor to the geographic interior (80– 90°S, 9.8306–9.8322 m/s²); and 170× as a whole-globe dimensionless lower bound. The only escape is to deny the H(r)↔g conditional, in which case the dome’s only quantitative gravity formula (Domain 4) is a WGS84 tautology with no dome-specific content — the fork produces the “self-contradicted” verdict. Domain 19 (Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary, r=0.92, “Sub-terrestrial structure”) carries only a one-sided “Anomaly <20 mGal” threshold that real East-Antarctic Bouguer values (−40 to −60 mGal) trivially exceed, so the threshold passes by construction. Post-hoc test fails (GRACE L2 products continuous since 2002; Pappa 2019, Ferraccioli 2011 are indexed pre-commit).

relevant_files: monitor.py NOT in scraped raw-text/ — claims reference V51.1 monitor.py archived elsewhere
docs/index.html (Part 2b generated from wins.json code_analysis tags)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 4 formula g(r)=9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(−r/λg)) with λg=8,619 km. At r=0 (where monitor.py evaluates), exp term is 1, yielding 9.8322 m/s² = WGS84 polar normal gravity. No dome-specific prediction emerges from this formula at its only evaluation point; the dome-specific 8,619 km decay length is exercised only if evaluated at r>0. Domain 19 runs an unphysical one-sided threshold comparison (<20 mGal PASS) against East-Antarctic Bouguer data that trivially falls outside the threshold, guaranteeing “PASS”-by-threshold not PASS-by-prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 4 formula has structure but evaluates at r=0 degenerate point = tautology. Domain 19 is threshold gate not numerical prediction. Current scraped raw-text/ doesn’t include monitor.py so exact monitoring semantics for WIN-067 must be inferred from live-power page; prior cycle reviews appear to have verified monitor.py directly. The wins.json code_analysis.monitoring = "none" is defensible as-is (no live fetch of Antarctic gravity data into the dome pipeline) but an adversarial defender could push for "hardcoded" given Domain 19’s threshold gate — we do not flag this as a c8 hole because the borderline call doesn’t materially change the argument and the current classification is the more conservative (weaker-for-us) label.
data_sources_used: GRACE / GRACE-FO L2 gravity products (JPL) — used by Pappa 2019, Shepherd 2012
WGS84 reference ellipsoid normal gravity formula (Somigliana)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 4 Falsification “Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard” — the falsification criterion is calibration to the globe, not a dome-specific test. Domain 19 threshold “Anomaly <20 mGal” is trivially exceeded by any honest East-Antarctic measurement — pass-by-threshold inverts the falsification logic.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None identified this cycle. The detail_evidence’s parenthetical "(V51.2 live-power page, Domain 4 equatorial gravity entry; identical formula in V51.1 monitor.py)" is appropriate given the scrape does not include monitor.py. Prior cycles (c4/c5/c6) verified the monitor.py claim directly.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: wins.json currently has monitoring="none". The scraped raw-text/ does not include monitor.py so we cannot re-verify a live fetch exists. Domain 4 (equatorial gravity) on the live-power page has a formula but the formula is WGS84-equivalent, and Domain 19 (Mascon Gravity) has only a one-sided threshold. No evidence of a live NASA/ESA gravimetry API call for WIN-067 in the currently scraped surface. "none" remains defensible.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The Antarctic gravity low is explained by standard geophysics — mantle plume + incomplete PGIA + dynamic topography from mantle convection — independently constrained by seismic imaging (Pappa 2019 JGR, Ferraccioli 2011 Nature). The dome invokes “Sub-terrestrial structure” (Domain 19, line 152) and “Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation” (Domain 4, line 69) as labels, with no equations, mechanisms, or physical content beyond the relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: GRACE Level-2 gravity products have been continuously published since the 2002 mission launch; Ferraccioli 2011 Nature East-Antarctic Bouguer compilation predates the dome model; Pappa 2019 JGR likewise. These are indexed public datasets plausibly known to the author at write-time, so the legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b does not apply. No pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author has been located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Domain 4 formula g(r)=9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(−r/8,619)) reuses WGS84 coefficients (9.7803 = WGS84 equatorial normal gravity, 0.005307 = WGS84 gravity flattening). At r=0 (monitor.py evaluation point) the exponential collapses to 1 and the formula reproduces WGS84 polar gravity exactly. The dome-specific decay length 8,619 km matches H(r) but is never exercised at a non-degenerate evaluation point by the dome’s own automation. Domain 19 is a threshold gate, not a derivation. No predicted gravity value is derived from dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height function, aetheric parameters) in a way that produces a number different from the globe answer.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c7 hole #1 UNADDRESSED by EXP-215 — in-detail 1,430× vs 170× asymmetry persists. The lead paragraph of detail_evidence concludes the apples-to-apples computation with "0.006 m/s² — a 1,430× error" and then offers the 170× dimensionless bound as a "conservative lower bound." The subsequent WGS84-tautology paragraph, however, concludes with "the formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×" — reverting to the 170× figure without the "lower bound" qualifier, and without noting that under the same apples-to-apples framing used 1500 characters earlier the miss is 1,430×. A careful reader lands on two different numbers for "how badly does the dome’s geometry fail" — 1,430× in paragraph 1, 170× in paragraph 4 — and no paragraph-level sentence reconciles them. EXP-215 deliberately scoped around this sentence (patch_instructions #5 says "do not change the ~1,430× headline that follows") but did not touch the closing "fails by 170×" line either. A sharp dome defender quoting the "fails by 170×" sentence can claim we’re inconsistent by a factor of 8.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch the closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph to read "...the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is dimensionless-fractional or apples-to-apples in m/s² at Antarctic latitude." OR change it to "fails by at least 170× (1,430× under apples-to-apples at Antarctic latitude; see paragraph above)." Either form closes the asymmetry without forcing a single-number choice.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c7 hole #6 ARITHMETIC PRECISION SLIP persists. detail_evidence states "the observed Antarctic gravity drop from the polar reference is only ~0.006 m/s² — a 1,430× error." 8.87 ÷ 0.006 = 1,478, not 1,430. The 1,430 figure is correct only at full precision: 9.8322 − 9.826 = 0.0062, and 8.868 ÷ 0.0062 = 1,430.3. The rounding from 0.0062 to 0.006 introduces a ~3% inconsistency that a hostile reader can exploit ("your own arithmetic doesn’t round-trip"). EXP-215 did not touch this. tldr_evidence carries the identical mismatch: "0.006 m/s² (0.063%)" — but 0.006 / 9.8322 = 0.061%, not 0.063%; the 0.063% figure requires 0.0062.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "~0.006 m/s²" with "0.0062 m/s²" throughout detail_evidence and tldr_evidence (the figure is quoted twice in detail_evidence and once in tldr_evidence). Alternative cosmetic fix: change the headline number to 1,478× (= 8.87/0.006 rounded), but the 0.0062 path is simpler because it preserves the round-tripping with the 0.063% percentage already used.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 hole #5 SHEPHERD 2012 CITATION PRECISION. detail_evidence cites "incomplete post-glacial isostatic adjustment from the Last Glacial Maximum ice sheet, measured directly by GRACE satellite gravimetry (Shepherd et al., 2012, Science)" with DOI 10.1126/science.1228102. That DOI resolves to "A Reconciled Estimate of Ice-Sheet Mass Balance" — a meta-analysis of Antarctic and Greenland ice mass trends using GRACE, ICESat, and altimetry. The paper uses GIA corrections in its mass-balance budgeting but is not primarily a characterisation of Antarctic post-glacial isostatic adjustment. A more precise citation for the PGIA claim would be Whitehouse et al. 2012 (Geophys. J. Int.) or King et al. 2012 (Nature). EXP-215 did not touch this citation. Severity is minor because the overall mechanism claim (PGIA contribution to Antarctic gravity low) is scientifically correct and widely documented; only the specific citation-to-claim mapping is loose.
holes_found.recommendation: Either swap the DOI for Whitehouse 2012 / King 2012 which are PGIA-primary, or soften the attribution to "constrained by GRACE satellite gravimetry (Shepherd et al., 2012, Science, used GRACE mass-balance products that depend on PGIA corrections)" — less crisp but accurate.

holes_found.description: DOMAIN 4 FALSIFICATION-VIOLATION CLAIM IS LOAD-BEARING BUT UNDERSTATED. detail_extra paragraph (iii) says "At r=20,015 km — the Antarctic rim, the only other geometrically-motivated evaluation point — Domain 4 predicts g ≈ 9.7854 m/s², which violates Domain 4’s own ‘Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard’ criterion by 4.8×." This is a strong argument that deserves more prominence. Computing: Domain 4 at r=20,015 gives 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(−20015/8619)) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·0.098) = 9.7803·1.000520 = 9.78539 m/s². WGS84 Somigliana at the South Pole (true dome r=20,015 correspondence) = 9.8322 m/s². Error = (9.8322 − 9.78539)/9.8322 = 0.476%. That’s 4.76× the 0.1% threshold, consistent with the text. But the argument is buried in paragraph (iii) of detail_extra which most readers will not reach. This is not a hole per se — it’s a strength-of-framing issue: our best self-contradiction proof (the dome’s own falsification criterion forces the claim to fail at the rim) is hidden in the defensive Q&A rather than in the main detail_evidence body.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider elevating one sentence of the Domain 4 r=20,015 falsification-violation computation into detail_evidence proper (currently only detail_extra (iii) carries it). A single sentence in the WGS84-tautology paragraph: "At the only other geometrically-motivated evaluation point r=20,015 km, Domain 4 predicts 9.785 m/s², which violates its own published falsification criterion by 4.8×." would make the self-contradiction visible without requiring the reader to expand the defensive Q&A block. Low-priority — the argument is sound as-is; promoting it strengthens presentation.

holes_found.description: ADVOCATE-MODE COUNTERARGUMENT: The dome’s Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary r=0.92 correlation is a genuine empirical regularity. Our detail text dismisses it as a "rebrand of a phenomenon that standard geophysics has explained since Ferraccioli 2011" but does not engage with why the correlation is statistically meaningful (r=0.92 is very high for any two global geophysical datasets). A strong dome defender could argue: "Standard geophysics explains the gravity low AND the SAA separately, but does it predict that the geographic footprint of the gravity low coincides with the SAA boundary at r=0.92? If not, the correlation is a novel finding that the dome flagged first, even if the mechanism (‘Sub-terrestrial structure’) is underspecified." Our current text does not rebut this specific argument. The likely answer — that shared mantle-structure causation produces the correlation as a consequence, not an independent prediction — deserves an explicit sentence.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short rebuttal to detail_evidence or detail_extra: "The r=0.92 Mascon-SAA correlation is not an independent dome-specific prediction because both fields are shaped by the same underlying mantle structure (East-Antarctic crustal roots + uncompensated mantle plume) — correlation is a downstream consequence of shared geological causation, not a novel forecast. Standard geophysics explains the co-location via common-cause reasoning without invoking dome mechanisms." Strengthens the rebuttal surface without changing the verdict.

stronger_arguments.description: Explicitly invoke the dome’s Domain 4 falsification text in the main detail_evidence body. The dome author’s published falsification criterion (Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard) combined with the fact that Domain 4 predicts 9.785 m/s² at r=20,015 km (0.48% error, i.e. 4.8× the author’s own threshold) is a self-executing contradiction: the author’s own rule forces the failure. Currently this argument lives only in detail_extra (iii), which is below the fold.

Add a one-line computation at r=20,015 km showing the Domain 4 formula’s prediction explicitly. Currently the text narrates “the exponential collapses at r=0” but does not compute the r=20,015 value in the main evidence body. A numerical cross-check makes the WGS84-tautology argument inescapable.

Cite the GRACE-FO Level-2 product catalog (https://grace.jpl.nasa.gov/data/grace-fo-products/) or the ICGEM service (https://icgem.gfz-potsdam.de/) as the operational confirmation that Antarctic gravity is continuously monitored at sub-mGal precision and matches WGS84 plus localised anomalies. This would harden the “observation is not novel” leg of the post-hoc argument.

kernel_of_truth.description: The Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary r=0.92 correlation IS a real statistical regularity, and the Antarctic gravity low is a real geophysical anomaly worth explaining. The dome author’s identification of a correlation between two independent anomaly fields is not trivially wrong — it reflects a shared underlying mantle structure that produces both signatures simultaneously. Additionally, the dome’s choice of 8,619 km as a characteristic length scale for aetheric phenomena is internally self-consistent across the model (H(r) decay, λg, etc.), even if the scale itself has no independent physical justification.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A correlation is not a mechanism. r=0.92 between two geophysical fields indicates shared causation, and standard geophysics supplies the shared cause (mantle plume beneath East Antarctica producing both crustal gravity low via isostasy AND ionospheric/magnetospheric asymmetry contributing to SAA). The dome’s contribution is to relabel this shared cause as "Sub-terrestrial structure" without supplying equations, numerical predictions, or measurable distinctions. Internal consistency of the 8,619 km length scale is a coherence property, not evidence; the model still predicts 90% gravity drop at the rim under any monotonic H(r)↔g coupling, which is falsified by 1,430× at Antarctic latitude and 170× dimensionless. The verdict Self-Contradicted stands: the dome must either (a) accept that H(r) drives gravity and fail by 170×–1,430×, or (b) deny the H(r)↔g link and admit Domain 4 is pure WGS84 with no dome content.

deeper_analysis: EXP-215 is a substantial, well-targeted patch package that directly resolves the three most serious unmet concerns from c6/c7 — finding-level linear-coupling disclosure, detail_evidence IF-THEN reframe, and geographic-anchor correction with explicit conservative-choice labeling. The IF-THEN construction is the single most important change: it converts what was a potentially straw-manned attribution ("dome claims g=f(H(r))") into a fork-of-a-dilemma that the dome author cannot escape without publishing a mechanism equation. Either horn of the fork produces a verdict ≤ Not Demonstrated for the dome’s WIN-067 claim. The c8 residuals are all either carry-forward items that EXP-215 deliberately scoped around (the in-detail 1,430× vs 170× asymmetry, the 0.006-vs-0.0062 rounding) or new concerns flagged for framing-strength rather than factual correctness. The Advocate-Mode concern (Mascon-SAA correlation not rebutted on its own terms) is the one substantively new observation — worth a single-sentence rebuttal but not verdict-affecting. Confidence rises from c7’s 0.85 to 0.88 because three real concerns are closed and two carry-forwards are aesthetic/precision rather than argument-structure; I would not call this 0.95 because the 170×/1,430× asymmetry has now persisted across two consecutive patch cycles and is a genuine reader-trap.

citations_failed: Shepherd et al. 2012 Science DOI 10.1126/science.1228102 — DOI resolves to "A Reconciled Estimate of Ice-Sheet Mass Balance", a meta-analysis of Antarctic/Greenland ice mass trends. Uses GRACE with GIA corrections but is not primarily a PGIA characterisation paper. The claim "incomplete post-glacial isostatic adjustment from the Last Glacial Maximum ice sheet, measured directly by GRACE satellite gravimetry" would be better supported by Whitehouse et al. 2012 or King et al. 2012. Severity minor — the mechanism claim is correct, only the citation-to-claim precision is loose.

Ferraccioli et al. 2011 Nature DOI 10.1038/nature09659 (no web access to verify this run)
